properties.remark: MT CHARLESTON NDF REPORTED A NUMBER OF ROCKS...SOME AS LARGE AS THE SIZE OF A PERSONS HEAD...WASHED ONTO DEER CREEK HIGHWAY FROM HEAVY RAIN EARLIER THIS MORNING.
BARSTOW-DAGGETT AIRPORT REPORTED 1/4 MILE VISIBILITY IN BLOWING DUST FROM 1210 PM UNTIL 1221 PM.
A SPOTTER REPORTED VISIBILITY AS LOW AS 3 FEET IN LANDERS FROM BLOWING DUST. TRAVEL ON AREA ROADS WAS REPORTED TO BE IMPOSSIBLE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED A LARGE ACCUMULATION OF SAND BLEW ONTO HARVARD ROAD RESULTING IN THE ROAD NEEDING TO BE CLOSED.
DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK PERSONNEL REPORTED BLOWING DUST AND SAND. VISIBILITY REDUCED TO LESS THAN A QUARTER OF MILE AT THE VISITORS CENTER.
A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED THAT VISIBILITY HAS BEEN REDUCED TO LESS THAN 10 FEET ON U.S. 95 NEAR THE ELDORADO DRY LAKE BED. VEHICLES HAVE BEEN PULLING OVER TO THE SIDE OF THE ROAD.

THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED MUD AND DEBRIS ON SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD...THE LOWER PORTION OF DANTES VIEW ROAD AND PORTIONS OF HIGHWAY 190. SEVERAL INCHES OF STANDING WATER WERE REPORTED ON BADWATER ROAD.
GUSTY WINDS FROM A DRY THUNDERSTORM KNOCKED OUT POWER TO AMBOY FOR ABOUT 3 HOURS AND DROPPED VISIBILITY TO ABOUT 100 YARDS FOR TWO HOURS.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL CLOSED STATE ROUTE 190 FROM HIGHWAY 395 ACROSS THE ENTIRE SOUTH END OF THE OWENS DRY LAKE BED DUE TO BLOWING DUST. VISIBILITY WAS REPORTED TO BE AS LOW AS ZERO IN THIS AREA.
OUTFLOW FROM THUNDERSTORMS THAT DISSIPATED TO THE EAST OF INDIAN SPRINGS.
DOLAN SPRINGS FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED HIGH WINDS REDUCED VISIBILITY TO ZERO WITH BROWNOUT CONDITIONS REPORTED. EVENT TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED STATE ROUTE 190 WAS CLOSED AT MILE MARKER 117 DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
DESERT NWR RAWS REPORTED SUSTAINED WINDS OF 45 AND A GUST TO 59 MPH FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
COUNTY ROAD 1 WAS CLOSED DUE TO HIGH WATER AND DEBRIS.
DRIVEWAY DAMAGED. ROCKS AND MUD WASHED ACROSS DRIVEWAY AND ROAD.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED A LARGE AMOUNT OF DEBRIS WASHED ONTO EMIGRANT CANYON ROAD.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS KNOCKED OVER A CONCRETE UTILITY POLE THAT TOOK OUT OTHER UTILITY EQUIPMENT IN THE AREA. SEVERAL TRAFFIC SIGNALS WERE BRIEFLY KNOCKED OUT.
NIPTON ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. THE EVENT TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
RUNNING WATER UP TO 8 INCHES DEEP ON THE ROAD CARRYING SMALL ROCKS.
MAJOR ROAD FLOODING WITH SOME ROADS CLOSED. SPOTTER RECEIVED .44 INCHES OF RAIN IN A 20 MINUTE PERIOD AND WOULD NOT ATTEMPT CROSSING A FLOODED ROADWAY IN A LARGE 4X4 VEHICLE.

FLASH FLOODING RESULTED IN A TOTAL OF 9 SWIFTWATER RESCUES IN LAKE HAVASU CITY INVOLVING AT LEAST 11 PEOPLE. A FEW HOMES WERE FLOODED. RUNOFF INTO WASHES FILLED ROTARY PARK WITH MUD AND UNDERMINED SIDEWALKS NEAR THE PARK. DEBRIS FROM WASHES FLOWED INTO LAKE HAVASU AND WAS FLOATING IN THE LAKE.
REPORTS OF SWIFT WATER RESCUES IN PROGRESS IN LAKE HAVASU CITY. CARS AND MOTORCYCLES SWEPT AWAY BY SWIFT MOVING WATER.
HIGHWAYS 62 AND 95 WERE FLOODED IN THE VICINITY OF VIDAL JUNCTION. A BIG RIG WAS STUCK IN THE MUD ON HIGHWAY 62 EIGHT MILES WEST OF THE JUNCTION.

SIGNIFICANT FLOODING AFFECTED HIGHWAY 62 FROM YUCCA VALLEY THROUGH AREAS EAST OF TWENTYNINE PALMS. WATER AND BOULDERS COVERED THE ROAD, PARTS OF THE ROAD AND SHOULDERS CAVED IN, AND NUMEROUS VEHICLES WERE STUCK. CONDITIONS IMPROVED AROUND 10 PM.
WATER FLOODED THE INTERSECTION OF KELBAKER AND KELSO CIMA ROADS.
CA HIGHWAY 190 WAS FLOODED AT MILE MARKER 84.
HIGHWAY 95 WAS CLOSED UNTIL APPROXIMATELY 1245 AM DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED A LARGE AMOUNT OF DEBRIS WASHED ONTO SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD FROM FLASH FLOODING.THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
COLORADO CITY 911 DISPATCH CENTER REPORTED 6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS A ROADWAY. ONE CAR IS STALLED IN THE ROADWAY. ROADWAY IS NOW CLOSED.
A SPOTTER REPORTED AT LEAST 12 INCHES OF WATER ON LOCAL STREETS NEAR HIGHWAY 62. HOMES HAD AS MUCH AS 5 INCHES OF WATER IN THEIR YARDS.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS APPROX. 6 INCHES OF WATER WITH SMALL GRAVEL FLOWING ACROSS PARK BLVD AND SR 62.
CO-OP OBSERVER AT DIAMOND M RANCH REPORTED 2.25 INCHES OF RAIN IN 40 MINUTES.
MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS STOCKTON ROAD AT MILE MARKER 38 IS CLOSED DUE TO WATER FLOWING ACROSS ROADWAY.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED PART OF NIPTON ROAD HAS BEEN WASHED AWAY NEAR INTERSTATE 15.
COUNTY ROAD 1 WAS CLOSED NEAR GOLDEN SHORES TO DEBRIS WASHING ONTO THE ROAD. THE ROAD WAS STILL CLOSED AS OF LATE SUNDAY MORNING.

MOHAVE COUNTY ROAD DEPARTMENT REPORTED ROUTE 66 WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING FROM MILE MARKER 1 THROUGH MILE MARKER 20 DUE TO DEBRIS COVERING THE ROAD. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS FROM MILE MARKER 6 THROUGH 20 WHERE SECTIONS OF PAVEMENT WERE MISSING.
REPORT FROM OATMAN FIRE DEPARTMENT THAT RESCUE VEHICLES COULD NOT CROSS FLOODED ROADWAY NEAR BOUNDARY CONE ROAD AND OATMAN HIGHWAY.

AT 824 PM PDT THE LAKE MEAD DISPATCH REPORTED THAT ROCK AND DEBRIS WAS OVER THE ROAD TO WILLOW BEACH AND THAT THE ROAD WOULD BE CLOSED OVERNIGHT. THERE IS POSSIBLE DAMAGE TO THE DOCK AREA. TIME OF THE EVENT IS ESTIMATED AS INFORMATION IS PRELIMINARY.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE WAS REPORTED ON WEST DEER SPRINGS WAY BETWEEN 7601 AND 7809.

A SPOTTER REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON EL CAPITAN BETWEEN GILMORE AND ALEXANDER THAT COVERED BOTH LANES. THE WATER ON GILMORE IS APPROXIMATE 4 INCHES DEEP AND THE WATER ON ALEXANDER IS APPROXIMATELY 8 INCHES DEEP. THREE AND HALF TO 5 INCH DIAMETER STONES COVERED THE ROAD.

3 TO 4 INCHES OF WATER COLLECTED ON DECATUR BOULEVARD NORTH ALONG WITH A SMALL AMOUNT OF MUD AND SMALL ROCKS JUST SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION WITH 215 NORTH. AROUND 4 INCHES OF WATER ON DECATUR BOULEVARD NEAR THE INTESECTION OF WASHINGTON ROAD WAS STILL ON THE GROUND NEARLY 10 HOURS AFTER THE RAIN ENDED.
3 FEET OF WATER ON BUFFALO DRIVE NEAR GRAND TEATON.
THE SAN BERNARDINO COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED MAJOR FLOODING ON US95 SOUTH AT 5 MILE ROAD. THE ROAD IS COMPLETELY WASHED OUT AND WAS CLOSED AT 1045 PM.
CHP REPORTS KELBAKER RD IS CURRENTLY FLOODED JUST SOUTH OF I15. REPORT ALSO INDICATES DEBRIS IN ROADWAY DUE TO FLOODING.
LINCOLN COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED BEAVER DAM ROAD HAD WASHED OUT NEAR HIGHWAY 93.
SEVERAL LARGE BOULDERS WASHED ONTO KYLE CANYON ROAD NEAR THE SPRING MOUNTAIN VISITOR CENTER.
THE LINCOLN COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED A PORTION OF HIGHWAY 93 WAS WASHED OUT WITH THE PAVEMENT REMOVED.
CO-OP OBSERVER IN RACHEL REPORTED THAT LAST NIGHT ON SR375 NEAR US6 THE ROAD WAS WASHED OUT.
SEVERAL SPOTTERS IN SUMMERLIN AND NORTH LAS VEGAS REPORTED HAIL FROM NICKEL TO SILVER DOLLAR SIZE BETWEEN 1025 PM AND 1042 PM. SOME VEHICLES WERE DAMAGED.
911 DISPATCH IN COLORADO CITY REPORTED LARGE TREES BEING REMOVED FROM BANKS OF THE SHORT CREEK DRAINAGE. OTHER LARGE DEBRIS HAS BEEN REPORTED IN THE FLOW.
WATER RUNNING FROM CURB TO CURB DOWN MAIN STREET IN PIOCHE AND WAS COMING UP OVER THE SIDEWALK AND INTO SOME BUSINESSES.

TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED FROM RADAR. ONGOING FLASH FLOODING WAS OBSERVED ALONG CIMA ROAD JUST SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 15 DURING STORM SURVEY AT 220 PM. 10-12 SIGNIFICANT DEBRIS FLOWS WERE OBSERVED ALONG CIMA ROAD 3 TO 5 MILES SOUTH OF INTERSTATE 15...SOME WITH LARGE DEBRIS BETWEEN 200 AND 220 PM. THE ROAD WAS NEARLY IMPASSABLE.

TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED BY RADAR. OBSERVED SIGNFICANT DEBRIS FLOW OVER KINGSTON RD ONE HALF MILE NORTH OF THE EXCELSIOR MINE FORK DURING STORM SURVEY AT 250 PM. THE ROAD WAS IMPASSABLE. SEVERAL LOCATIONS...8 TO 9 MILES NORTH OF INTERSTATE 15 ALONG EXCELSIOR MINE ROAD HAD DEBRIS FLOWS...OBSERVED BETWEEN 240 AND 245 PM DURING SURVEY. THE FLOODING HAD ENDED AT ALL OF THESE LOCATIONS AT THE TIME OF THE SURVEY.
AN AUTOMATED WEATHER STATION LOCATED AT SIERRA VISTA HIGH SCHOOL AT THE INTERSECTION OF CIMMARON AND ROBINDALE REPORTED A WIND GUST OF 62 MPH.
THE AREA 9 SENSOR ON THE NEVADA NATIONAL SECURITY SITE RECORDED A GUST OF 60 MPH FROM THE SOUTHEAST. THE SENSOR IS LOCATED 33 MILES NORTH OF MERCURY.
A TRAINED WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED THAT HIS DAVIS WEATHER STATION RECORDED A GUST TO 84 MPH AS A ROLLING WALL OF DUST IMPACTED HIS HOUSE.
THE BLM REPORTED THAT SEVERAL BACK COUNTRY ROADS WERE WASHED OUT AND IMPASSABLE IN THE GRAND CANYON-PARASHANT NATIONAL MONUMENT. THE TIME OF FLASH FLOODING WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS DEBRIS ON AMBOY ROAD DUE TO FLOODING.
A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD AND SMALL ROCKS WASHED ONTO THE 215 BELTWAY AT LONE MOUNTAIN ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS FIVE OR MORE VEHICLES STUCK IN WASH 20 MILES EAST OF TWENTYNINE PALMS ON STATE ROUTE 62.
TRAINED SPOTTER IN WONDER VALLEY CA REPORTED 6+ INCHES OF WATER MOVING ACROSS GAEDKE LANE IN WONDER VALLEY. SPOTTER ALSO MEASURED 0.33 INCHES FELL IN 10 MINUTES.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROADWAY FLOODING ON STATE ROUTE 62 AND HIGHWAY 95 BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 104 AND 110. STATE ROUTE 62 HAS BEEN CLOSED WEST OF VIDAL JUNCTION.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS PARTS OF KELBAKER ROAD NEAR I-40 ARE COVERED WITH MUD AND ROCKS.
BULLHEAD CITY POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED THE LAUGHLIN-BULLHEAD CITY BRIDGE ROAD FLOODED. NUMEROUS ROADS IN BULLHEAD CITY WERE FLOODED WITH SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER AND COVERED WITH ROCKS AND MUD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS FLOODING ACROSS ROADWAY APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES NORTH OF I-40 ON US 95.
CASINO RIVER DRIVE IN LAUGHLIN WAS FLOODED WITH AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF WATER.
A SPOTTER REPORTED BOULDERS IN THE ROAD ALONG HIGHWAY 66. PORTIONS OF THE ROAD FROM BULLHEAD CITY TO OATMAM HAS BEEN WASHED OUT. IN ADDITION THE SPOTTER RECEIVED 1.80 INCHES OF RAIN IN ABOUT 1 HOUR.
LAUGHLIN FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED WATER WASHED DEBRIS ONTO THE ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF BRUCE WOODBURY DRIVE AND THOMAS EDISON DRIVE.
LAS VEGAS BROADCAST MEDIA REPORTED AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF MUDDY FLOWING WATER AT THE INTERSECTION WAGON WHEEL AND BOULDER HIGHWAY.
CHP REPORTS AMBOY RD CLOSED AT SHEEPHOLE VW DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. MORE THAN ONE VEHICLE IS STUCK IN THE WATER.
THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED MODERATE TO SEVERE DAMAGE TO RACETRACK ROAD. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
THUNDERSTORMS WINDS KNOCKED DOWN 5 POWER POLES AND SEVERAL TREES IN TRONA.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT A 75 FOOT LONG SECTION OF STATE ROUTE 190 WAS COVERED WITH 2 FEET OF MUD NEAR TOWNES PASS IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS CAUSED 2000 CUSTOMERS TO LOSE POWER IN THE TOWN OF TRONA.
ESTIMATED 8 TO 12 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS TRONA ROAD.
LEMOIGNE CANYON ROAD HAD SEVERE FLOOD DAMAGE ON THE FIRST QUARTER MILE OF THE ROAD. COTTONWOOD CANYON ROAD HAD DEEP MUD AND STANDING WATER ON IT. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED MAJOR FLOOD DAMAGE TO DARWIN FALLS ROAD IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK.
BEATTY DISPATCH REPORTED LARGE TREE BRANCH DOWN JUST SOUTH OF BEATTY ON HIGHWAY 95.
SEVERAL LARGE TREE LIMBS WERE KNOCKED DOWN BY A THUNDERSTORM IN BEATTY.
A SPOTTER REPORTED THE WEST END OF NORTH AVENUE IN BEATTY WHICH IS A GRAVEL ROAD WASHED OUT. THE EAST END OF NORTH AVENUE WHICH IS PAVED HAD MUD ON IT. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
NEVADA TREASURE RV RESORT REPORTED MUD WASHED ONTO A NUMBER OF ROADS IN THE RESORT AND SEVERAL INCHES OF STANDING WATER WERE STILL AROUND ON SATURDAY IN SPOTS. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
PUBLIC REPORT OF SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER AND MUD ON HIGHWAY 160 NORTH OF PAHRUMP. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
THE NYE COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED THAT MUD WASHED ONTO PORTIONS OF ASH MEADOWS ROAD. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS MAJOR FLOODING WITH TWO OCCUPIED VEHICLES STUCK IN FLOODING ALONG STATE ROUTE 190 NEAR DANTES VIEW.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED MAJOR FLOODING ALONG STATELINE ROAD EAST OF DEATH VALLEY JUNCTION.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED SEVERAL AREAS OF DANTES VIEW ROAD WASHED OUT. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.

THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED BADWATER ROAD HAD DEBRIS ON THE ROAD FROM MILE POST 8 TO 15. IN ADDITION NATURAL BRIDGE ROAD...DEVILS GOLF COURSE AND 20 MULE TEAM ROAD WERE CLOSED DUE TO WASHOUTS. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED CLOSURE OF STATE ROUTE 190 JUST WEST OF DANTES VIEW DUE TO FLOODING AND A TELEPHONE POLE DOWN BETWEEN MILE MARKER 128 1ND 130.
MUD AND DEBRIS WASHED ONTO THE PAVED ROAD TO THE CHARCOAL KILNS IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS BLEW TRAMPOLINES INTO POWER LINES IN PEACH SPRINGS.
DOLAN SPRINGS FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED 3 POWER POLES WERE KNOCKED DOWN IN DOLAN SPRINGS BY WIND FROM A THUNDERSTORM.
PIERCE FERRY ROAD WAS SHUT DOWN TO DUE TO FLOODING AT MILE POST 15. TOURISTS FROM GRAND CANYON WEST WERE HALTED IN THEIR VEHICLES DUE TO THE FLOODING.
MUD WASHED ONTO PORTIONS OF DIAMOND CREEK ROAD.
A SPOTTER REPORTED VISIBILITY REDUCED TO ONE MILE IN BLOWING DUST ON HIGHWAY 93 NEAR CHLORIDE.
STOCKTON HILL ROAD WAS FLOODED AND CLOSED.

A SPOTTER MEASURED A 68 MPH WIND GUST AT A HOME WEATHER STATION. THE WIND WAS SHOWING A STEADY PERIOD OF 50 TO 55 MPH WINDS. THE WIND KNOCKED OVER A LARGE MESQUITE TREE AND PUNCHED A HOLE IN THE SIDE OF HIS HOUSE.
AN 8 BY 12 FOOT WINDOW WAS BLOWN OUT OF THE RIVERSIDE CASINO.

BULLHEAD CITY POLICE DEPARTMENT REPORTED NUMEROUS POWER LINES DOWN AND POWER POLES KNOCKED DOWN. POWER WAS OUT TO A CONSIDERABLE PORTION OF BULLHEAD CITY. ROOFS WERE AKEN OFF A NUMBER OF MOBILE HOMES. SHEDS WERE KNOCKED OVER AND AWNINGS WERE OFF MOBILE HOMES.
A SPOTTER REPORTED 2 BIG TREES DOWN IN THE SAMS CLUB PARKING LOT IN BULLHEAD CITY. ONE CAR WINDSHIELD WAS BROKEN OUT.
POWER WAS KNOCKED OUT TO RESIDENTIAL NEIGHBORHOODS ON THE NORTH SIDE OF LAUGHLIN. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
A REPORT WAS RECEIVED OF 6 INCHES OF WATER CONTAINING ROCK AND DEBRIS ACROSS THE ROAD AT THE INTERSECTION OF TROPICANA AND STEPHANIE.
SMALL AMOUNTS OF MUD WASHED ONTO HUALAPAI MOUNTAIN ROAD BETWEEN THE 5500 AND 6000 FOOT ELEVATION. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED WINDS OF 25 MPH ALONG WITH VISIBILITY AT 1/4 MILE DUE TO BLOWING DUST IN BULLHEAD CITY.
VISIBILITY WAS LESS THAN A QUARTER OF A MILE DUE TO BLOWING DUST IN TOPOCK. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED 35 MPH.
VISIBILITY WAS ESTIMATED AT LESS THAN 30 FEET DUE TO BLOWING DUST IN LAUGHLIN.
A SPOTTER ESTIMATED 40 TO 50 MPH WIND GUSTS IN NEEDLES. FULLY LOADED NEWSPAPER STANDS WERE KNOCKED OVER. VISIBILITY REDUCED TO ABOUT 2 BLOCKS...OR 1/4 MILE.
69 MPH GUST REPORTED AT BULLHEAD CITY CW4367 MESONET STATION.

THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS CAUSED SEVERAL TENTS TO COLLAPSE DURING A PARTY AT THE COMMUNITY PARK IN BULLHEAD CITY. THE WIND ALSO KNOCKED OVER SEVERAL PORTABLE TOILETS AND BROKE OFF MULTIPLE TREE BRANCHES.
POWER LINES WERE REPORTED DOWN IN GOLDEN SHORES AND MOHAVE VALLEY. VISIBILITY BETWEEN 50 AND 500 FEET.
DAMAGE TO FENCES AND SIGNS FROM BULLHEAD CITY AND MOHAVE VALLEY. POWER WAS OUT IN OLD BULLHEAD AND IN MOHAVE VALLEY.
POWER WAS KNOCKED OUT IN DOWNTOWN KINGMAN.
TIKABOO PEAK MESONET SITE MEASURED A 59 MPH THUNDERSTORM WIND GUST.
6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING DOWN THE CENTER OF BUENA VISTA DRIVE.
BANK STREET BY HIGH SCHOOL COMPLETELY UNDER WATER. WATER IS OVER THE CURB ON BOTH SIDES OF THE ROAD.
REPORT OF TWO TREES DOWN ACROSS SIDEWALK AND PARTIALLY IN THE ROADWAY NEAR AIRWAY AND HEATHER.
6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ON AVALON AVE BETWEEN ABERDEEN DRIVE AND WINTERS DRIVE IN LANDERS. WATER CONTAINS ROCKS AND DEBRIS.
WINDS CAUSED A LARGE CAMPER TO TIP OVER. WIND GUST ALSO REMOVED SEVERAL FENCE POLES FROM THE GROUND. SEVERE WINDS LASTED FOR A 10 MINUTE PERIOD STARTING AT 1620 PDT.
8-10 INCHES OF SWIFT FLOWING WATER AT THE INTERSECTION OF YUCCA MESA RD AND BUENA VISTA DRIVE IN YUCCA VALLEY.
THE PARK SERVICE REPORTED HEAVY RAIN WASHED OUT PARTS OF MOSAIC CANYON ROAD IN DEATH VALLEY NATIONAL PARK.
FLASH FLOODING REPORTED BY CHP AT INTERSECTION OF ROUTES 190 AND 136. VEHICLES NOT ABLE TO PASS. A LARGE BOULDER WAS IN THE ROADWAY.
SEVERAL INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ALONG WITH NUMEROUS ROCKS AND AREAS OF MUD WERE ON ROADS NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF ARROYO GRANDE AND HORIZON RIDGE IN HENDERSON.

THE SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTED FOUR TO FIVE FEET OF WATER FLOWING OUT OF CLOVER CANYON INTO CLOVER CREEK. HIGHWAY 317 WAS CLOSED. NO DAMAGE WAS REPORTED IN CALIENTE, BUT WATER WAS STILL FLOWING AS OF 840 PM.
WATER AT LEAST SIX TO EIGHT INCHES DEEP WAS FLOWING THROUGH THE INTERSECTION OF WASHINGTON AND MICHAEL.
4 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WITH ROCKS AND MUD REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF JO RAE AND JONES. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
FLOWING WATER WITH ROCKS AND MUD REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION OF RAINBOW AND LANDBERG. TIME ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
4-6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WITH ROCKS AND MUD REPORTED AT THE INTERSECTION SLIVERADO RANCH AT DECATUR. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS THAT HIGHWAY 190 IS WASHED OUT AT MILE MARKER 79 DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. HIGHWAY 190 IS CLOSED DUE TO THE WASHOUT.
HAIL FELL FOR 10 MINUTES STARTING AT 1420.
10 INCHES OF WATER AND MUD FLOWING DOWN BRIGADE DRIVE.
WIND LASTED FOR ABOUT 10 MINUTES.
THUNDERSTORM WINDS OF 70 MPH LASTED FOR 5 MINUTES IN WIKIEUP. WINDS CAUSED A PERGOLA TO TIP OVER.
PUBLIC REPORTED THAT SEVERAL ROADS ON THE WAY TO THE BAR TEN RANCH WERE WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED LOTS OF MUD AND DEBRIS AT CHICKEN SPRINGS ROAD AND US 93. THEY ALSO STATED THAT ADOT WAS ON THE WAY TO CLEAR THE ROAD.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED VERY HEAVY RAIN WITH A LOT OF WATER AND DEBRIS ON HUALAPAI ROAD BETWEEN MILE MARKERS 5 AND 6. THE ROAD WAS REPORTED AS IMPASSABLE FOR LOW CLEARANCE VEHICLES.
SEVERAL TREES WERE UPROOTED IN KINGMAN BY THUNDERSTORM WINDS. POWER LINES AND CABLE LINES WERE ALSO KNOCKED DOWN. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
THE HUALAPAI LODGE REPORTED THAT DIAMOND CREEK ROAD WAS COMPLETELY WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING FROM THE PEACH SPRINGS TURNOFF ALL THE WAY TO THE COLORADO RIVER. THE ROAD HAS BEEN CLOSED.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED WATER FLOWING OVER US-93 SOUTH OF COYOTE SPRINGS. ROADWAY HAS BECOME IMPASSIBLE TO VEHICLES.
TRAINED SPOTTERS REPORTED SMALL BUSHES AND ROCKS BEING PUSHED ACROSS US-93 SOUTH OF COYOTE SPRINGS. THE ROADWAY IS IMPASSIBLE AND WILL REQUIRE D.O.T. EQUIPMENT TO BE CLEARED.

PUBLIC REPORTED FENCE AND HOUSE DAMAGE DUE TO STRONG WINDS. PARTY STATED THAT 8 TO 10 POUND ROCKS WERE BEING THROWN INTO HIS HOUSE CAUSING DAMAGE TO THE STUCCO. PARTY ALSO STATED THAT THE FENCE SURROUNDING HIS CATTLE PEN WAS BLOWN DOWN DUE TO THE WIND. NO WIND ESTIMATION WAS GIVEN. ESTIMATED DAMAGE TO PROPERTY WAS APPROXIMATELY 1,500 DOLLARS.
ONE AND ONE HALF FEET OF FLOOD WATER OVER STATE ROUTE 319 AT MILE MARKER 70 NEAR THE UTAH STATE LINE.
6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WITH MUD AND ROCKS AT THE INTERSECTION OF POLARIS AVE AND SERENE AVE.
3 INCHES OF WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS SANDY VALLEY ROAD.
A 4 MILE SECTION OF STATE ROUTE 266 NEAR LIDA SUMMITT WAS WASHED OUT AND SUSTAINED SERIOUS DAMAGE FROM A FLASH FLOOD. ROAD REPAIR CREWS WERE ABLE TO REOPEN THE ROAD ABOUT 7 HOURS LATER.
ROADS WERE FLOODED IN SANDY VALLEY.
4 INCHES OF WATER WITH MUD AND ROCKS FLOWING ACROSS WARM SPRINGS ROAD JUST WEST OF BUFFALO DRIVE.
THE MID HILLS CAMPGROUND WAS CUT OFF BY WIDESPREAD FLASH FLOODING. CAMPERS MAY BE STRANDED.
THE CITY OF HENDERSON REPORTS THAT THE FOLLOWING INTERSECTIONS IN HENDERSON ARE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING...SUNRIDGE HEIGHTS AT SEVEN HILLS...ANNABELLE AT WARM SPRINGS...AND...SUNSET AT ARROYO GRANDE.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT WATER FLOWED OVER STATE ROUTE 62 NEAR SUNBURST STREET LEAVING MUD AND DEBRIS ON THE HIGHWAY.
REPORT OF UP TO 1 FOOT OF WATER RUNNING DOWN ROADWAY IN SLOAN.

*** 1 FATAL *** THE CLARK COUNTY CORONER OFFICE CONFIRMED A 17 YEAR OLD BOY DIED AFTER HE FELL INTO A WASH NEAR STEPHANIE AND SUNSET IN HENDERSON. HIS BODY WAS FOUND ON AUGUST 24TH IN THE WETLANDS WEST OF BROADBENT BOULEVARD.
ONE PERSON WAS STRANDED IN FAST MOVING WATER JUST SOUTH OF THE HOLE IN THE WALL CAMPGROUND.
PARK SERVICE REPORTS THAT MULTIPLE ROADS INSIDE VALLEY OF FIRE STATE PARK ARE CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING ACROSS ROADWAYS.

SEVERAL UNPAVED ROADS WERE REPORTED TO BE CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING IN THE SOUTHEAST PORTION OF THE MOJAVE NATIONAL PRESERVE. SECTIONS OF PAVED ROADS WERE COVERED WITH DEBRIS AND GRAPEFRUIT SIZED ROCKS. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
ROCKS AND DEBRIS WERE WASHED ACROSS THE BAILEY ROAD OFFRAMP FROM INTERSTATE 15 NORTHBOUND.
6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WITH MUD AND ROCKS AT THE INTERSECTION OF POLARIS AVE AND SERENE AVE.
PARK SERVICE EMPLOYEE REPORTED 6 TO 8 INCHES OF WATER WERE RUNNING ACROSS NORTHSHORE ROAD AT MILE MARKER 7.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT AMBOY ROAD NEAR SHADOW MOUNTAIN ROAD WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
TRAINED SPOTTER IN TWENTYNINE PALMS REPORTS LARGE ROCKS AND DEBRIS WITH WATER RUNNING ACROSS STATE ROUTE 62 IN MUTLIPLE LOCATIONS FROM AMBOY ROAD TO GODWIN ROAD.
THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED DEBRIS OVER TEMPLE BAR ROAD. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
I 15 EXIT 75 IS WASHED OUT DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
MULTIPLE STREETS IN BOULDER CITY ARE CLOSED DUE TO WATER AND DEBRIS.
HENDERSON AIRPORT RD FROM THE TERMINAL TO VOLUNTEER BLVD HAS BEEN CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
A CHURCH LOCATED AT CACTUS AND BERMUDA WAS DAMAGED WHEN FLOOD WATERS ENTERED THE BUILDING. 3 FEET OF WATER ENTERED THE CHURCH CAUSING OVER 500,000 DOLLARS IN DAMAGE.
A CHURCH LOCATED AT CACTUS AND BERMUDA WAS DAMAGED WHEN FLOOD WATERS ENTERED THE BUILDING.
1 FOOT OF WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS GOFFS RD. A TRUCK IS STUCK IN THE WATER DUE TO THE FLOODING.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT SR 136 WAS CLOSED DUE TO FLASH FLOODING BETWEEN US 395 AND SR 190. TIME WAS ESTIMATED BASED RADAR DATA.
THE NATIONAL PARK SERVICE REPORTED THAT SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD WAS WASHED OUT WITH SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO THE PAVEMENT.
WHITE DOME ROAD WAS UNDERCUT AND MOST OF IT WAS COMPLETELY WASHED OUT. MUD AND DEBRIS WAS OBSERVED OVER ROADS AT 10 DIFFERENT SPOTS WITHIN VALLEY OF FIRE STATE PARK.
DEATH VALLEY PARK RANGERS REPORTED FLASH FLOODING ON SCOTTYS CASTLE ROAD BETWEEN SCOTTYS CASTLE AND THE PARK BOUNDARY. THE ROAD TO UBEHEBE CRATER WAS ALSO CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT HIGHWAY 95 WAS CLOSED DUE TO WATER AND DEBRIS FLOWING ACROSS THE ROAD NEAR MILE MARKER 70 AND NEAR MILE MARKER 61.
3 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER WITH SMALL ROCKS AT THE INTERSECTION OF HUALAPAI MOUNTAIN ROAD AND EASTERN STREET IN KINGMAN.
CHP REPORTS THAT US 95 IS IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING JUST SOUTH OF THE CA NV BORDER. A SEMI IS STALLED IN THE MIDDLE OF THE ROADWAY.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED SWIFT MOVING WATER RUNNING ACROSS HUALAPAI MOUNTAIN ROAD BETWEEN MILE MARKER 9 AND 10 WITH LARGE ROCKS AND DEBRIS IN THE FLOW.
THE RED ROCK SCENIC LOOP WAS FLOODED AND CLOSED. ONE VEHICLE WAS NEARLY SWEPT AWAY.
ESTIMATED 60 MPH WIND GUST REPORTED BY A GAS STATION IN KRAMER JUNCTION.
HIGHWAY 62 FROM GODWIN ROAD TO HIGHWAY 177 HAS BEEN SHUT DOWN DUE TO FLOODING. THE CLOSURE DISTANCE IS ABOUT 44 MILES. THERE ARE SEVERAL AREAS OF FLOWING WATER OVER THE HIGHWAY.
REPORT OF ONE FOOT OF WATER FLOWING OVER BOTH BONNIE SPRINGS ROAD AND THE SPRING MOUNTAIN RANCH STATE PARK ACCESS ROAD JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 159.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL RECOMMENDED CLOSURE OF HIGHWAY 95 DUE TO SEVERAL WASHES RUNNING ACROSS THE ROAD. THE CLOSURE WAS FROM LAKE HAVASU ROAD TO VIDAL JUNCTION.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTED THAT HIGHWAY 95 WAS CLOSED FROM APPROXIMATELY MILE MARKER 21 TO VIDAL JUNCTION DUE TO FLOODING AT MILE MARKER 29. THIS IS WHERE THE CHEMEHUEVI WASH CROSSES THE HIGHWAY.

CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL HAS CLOSED HIGHWAY 95 DUE TO FLASH FLOODING AT MILE MARKER 29 SOUTH OF NEEDLES. HIGHWAY PATROL STATED THAT THE WATER WAS TOO DEEP FOR CARS TO DRIVE THROUGH AND THERE WAS DEBRIS ALL OVER THE ROAD.

*** 1 INJ *** FRONT WINDOWS BROKEN OUT OF RETAIL BUSINESS. MINOR INJURIES WITH ONE PERSON HOSPITALIZED. OTHER REPORTS OF TREES AND 4 POWER POLES DOWN ACROSS MOHAVE VALLEY. POWER TRANSFORMER REMOVED FROM POLE. TIME ESTIMATED.
THE CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL WEBSITE REPORTED ROADWAY FLOODING WITH VEHICLES STUCK ON HIGHWAY 62 EAST OF TWENTYNINE PALMS NEAR MILE MARKER 40.
TRAINED SPOTTER IN MOHAVE VALLEY ESTIMATED WIND GUST AT 80 MPH.

TRAINED SPOTTER IN NEEDLES REPORTED SEVERAL LARGE PINE TREES AND SEVERAL POWER POLES BLOWN DOWN BY THE WIND. SPOTTER ALSO STATED THAT THE ROOF OF THE NEEDLES MOVIE THEATER LOCATED AT W. BROADWAY AND F STREET HAD ITS ROOF COMPLETELY BLOWN OFF. WINDS WERE ESTIMATED OVER 80 MPH.

*** 1 INJ *** FRONT WINDOWS BROKEN OUT OF RETAIL BUSINESS. MINOR INJURIES WITH ONE PERSON HOSPITALIZED. OTHER REPORTS OF TREES AND 4 POWER POLES DOWN ACROSS MOHAVE VALLEY. POWER TRANSFORMER REMOVED FROM POLE. TIME ESTIMATED.

A TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED WIND GUSTS UP TO 60 MPH CAUSING THE ROOF TO BECOME SEPARATED FROM AT LEAST 1 HOME AND DOWNING 3 POWER POLES. THE SPOTTER ALSO REPORTED HEAVY RAIN WITH 6 INCHES OF WATER ON BROADWAY STREET.
THE MOHAVE COUNTY SHERIFF REPORTED PLANTATION DRIVE IN MOHAVE VALLEY WAS CLOSED DUE TO A FLASH FLOOD. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
SEVERAL MAINTAINED DIRT ROADS WASHED OUT NEAR OLD HIGHWAY 93. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.
HENDERSON FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED SEVERAL INCHES OF WATER FLOODED SUNRIDGE HEIGHTS AND CORONADO CENTER DRIVE AS WELL AS VOLUNTEER BOULEVARD AND EXECUTIVE AIRPORT ROAD.

LAS VEGAS AREA BROADCAST MEDIA HAD PICTURES SHOWING AT LEAST 6 INCHES OF FLOWING WATER ON SUNRISE HEIGHTS PARKWAY BETWEEN SEVEN HILLS AND PECOS RIDGE. ALSO A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD...ROCKS AND FLOWING WATER WASHED THROUGH THE PARKING LOT OF AN APARTMENT COMPLEX IN SEVEN HILLS.
CHP REPORTED DEBRIS AND WATER RUNNING ACROSS HIGHWAY 95 NORTH OF INTERSTATE 40.

TRAINED SPOTTER IN GOLDEN VALLEY REPORTED SEVERAL ROADS COMPLETELY FLOODED AND IMPASSABLE WITH AT LEAST TWO FEET OF WATER COVERING THE ROADS. SPOTTER ALSO STATED IT WAS THE WORST HE HAS EVER SEEN.
ROCKS AND DIRT WERE OBSERVED ACROSS DEER CREEK HIGHWAY. SEVERAL SECONDARY ROADS OFF OF LEE CANYON ROAD WERE REPORTED TO BE WASHED OUT. THE TIME OF OCCURRENCE WAS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR DATA.
LOCAL LAW ENFORCEMENT REPORTED THAT DIAMOND CREEK ROAD WAS IMPASSABLE DUE TO FLOODING.

ESMERALDA COUNTY SHERIFFS OFFICE REPORTS MUD AND DEBRIS OVER HIGHWAY 95 DUE TO FLASH FLOODING. TIME IS ESTIMATED.

A SMALL AMOUNT OF RUNNING WATER MOVED ACROSS LONE MOUNTAIN ROAD JUST EAST OF 215 ALONG WITH MUD AND SMALL ROCKS. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.

THE CLARK COUNTY REGIONAL JUSTICE CENTER WAS CLOSED DUE TO WATER ENTERING AND FLOODING THE BUILDING. TWO MACHINES USED TO SCAN FOR WEAPONS WERE DAMAGED BY FLOODWATERS AS WELL AS SEVERAL SECURITY CAMERAS.
REPORT OF 4 TO 6 INCHES OF WATER ACROSS INTERSECTION OF BONANZA AND PECOS NO DEBRIS BUT WATER IS FLOWING OVER CURBS.
SKYWARN RADIO OPERATOR RELAYED A REPORT OF CARS STALLED DUE TO FLOODING AT DECATUR AND JUST SOUTH OF OAKEY ROAD.
THE SPOTTER REPORTED 7 INCHES OF MOVING WATER COVERING ONE WESTBOUND LANE ON HIGHWAY 215 NEAR LAS VEGAS BLVD.
SKYWARN RADIO OPERATOR RELAYED A REPORT OF CARS STALLED DUE TO FLOODING AT DECATUR AND JUST SOUTH OF OAKEY ROAD.
NEVADA HIGHWAY PATROL CLOSED 215 FROM I-15 TO WARM SPRINGS. THE AIRPORT CONNECTOR WAS CLOSED DUE TO A LARGE AMOUNT OF MUD AND ROCKS THAT WASHED DOWN THE SIDES OF THE ROAD FROM LANDSCAPED AREAS.

NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTS LARGE AMOUNT OF WATER MOVING ACROSS ARROYO GRANDE BETWEEN HORIZON RIDGE PARKWAY AND 215. WATER ALSO FLOWING ACROSS HORIZON RIDGE NEAR THE POST OFFICE.
SPOTTER REPORTS THAT THE INTERSECTION OF WARM SPRINGS AND EASTERN IS IMPASSABLE. ALSO REPORTS AT LEWIS AND CASINO CENTER 3 TO 4 INCHES OF WATER IS FLOWING ACROSS ROAD.

REPORT FROM THE NHP THAT STATE ROUTE 169 IN OVERTON IS CLOSED NEAR THE OVERTON WASH.

SPOTTER REPORTED MULTIPLE ROAD CLOSURES IN LOGANDALE. GUBLER ROAD AND HEYER IS CLOSED.

10 BUSINESSES SUSTAINED SIGNIFICANT FLOOD DAMAGE IN THE PROMENADE SHOPPING CENTER ON MARYLAND PARKWAY NEAR UNLV. MUD WAS HALFWAY UP THE WALLS OF BUSINESSES. SOME BUSINESSES REPORTED COMPLETE DAMAGE TO FURNITURE AND EQUIPMENT. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.

A PARKING LOT ON THE CAMPUS OF UNLV FLOODED NEAR THE THOMAS AND MACK CENTER. PHOTOS SHOWED AT LEAST 2 TO 3 DOZEN VEHICLES WERE SUBMERGED IN SEVERAL FEET OF MUDDY WATER. SOME CARS WERE FILLED WITH WATER INSIDE.

CLARK COUNTY FIREFIGHTERS RESCUED AT LEAST 50 PEOPLE FROM THEIR VEHICLES IN THE LAS VEGAS VALLEY WITH A TOTAL OF 40 SWIFTWATER RESCUE CALLS. 15 PEOPLE WERE RESCUED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF SAHARA AND SLOAN WITH ONE RESCUE DONE BY HELICOPTER.
NHP REPORTS THAT INTERSTATE 15 IS CLOSED AT MILE MARKER 75. THIS IS WHERE THE CALIFORNIA WASH CROSSES I-15.
NHP REPORTS THAT INTERSTATE 15 IS CLOSED AT MILE MARKER 75. THIS IS WHERE THE CALIFORNIA WASH CROSSES I-15.

UP TO AN INCH OF WATER INSIDE THE TBE BUILDING ON THE CAMPUS OF UNLV DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.
UP TO 2 INCHES OF WATER INSIDE THE MGM GRAND GARDEN ARENA DUE TO FLASH FLOODING.

AT LEAST 45 HOMES ON AND NEAR WALTON HEATH AVENUE WERE FLOODED BY 4 TO 5 FEET OF WATER. A NUMBER OF VEHICLES WERE FLOODED AND SOME WERE FLOATED 300 TO 400 FEET AWAY. 3 DOGS DIED AFTER THEY DROWNED IN THE FLOODWATERS. SEVERAL CONDOS ALSO FLOODED IN ANOTHER AREA OF EAST LAS VEGAS AFTER FLOODWATERS BUSTED THROUGH A WALL.
A WORKER AT THE DESERT GOLF COURSE WAS SWEPT AWAY BY FLOODWATERS AND IS MISSING. HIS BULLDOZER WAS FOUND FULL OF MUD. THE EVENT TIME IS AN ESTIMATE.

*** 1 FATAL *** A LANDSCAPER WORKING AT THE DESERT ROSE GOLF COURSE IN EAST LAS VEGAS WAS SWEPT AWAY BY FLOOD WATERS. HIS BODY WAS DISCOVERED ON THE AFTERNOON OF SEPTEMBER 13 ABOUT TWO AND HALF MILES FROM WHERE HE WAS LAST SEEN.
